title: Spectrophotometric determination of cerium(IV) using potassium ferrocyanide as an analytical reagent
author: Amita Garg
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Kurukshetra University, Kurukshetra-136 119, Haryana, India 

  Manuscript received 14 February 2005, revised 21 March 2006, accepted 27 March 2006  

 Potassium ferrocyanide is used in the determination of cerium(IV) spectrophotometrically. It forms a greenish yellow complex in dilute acetic acid solution with  \(\lambda_{max}\)  at 425 nm. The method is free from interference of a large number of metal ions. Molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity of the formed complex are 6.0 x 10 3  L mol -1  cm -1  and 0.023 μg cm -2 , respectively.
